title: Aliasing, Effective Type, Provenance, and Type Punning · Stakeholder Native Systems Mission
summary: A small network monitoring team. The team needs a binary packet transformer with checked sizes, tagged representations, overlap-safe moves, no stale aliases, defined initialization, and optimization-stable decoding. Planned artifact: Deliver Memory-Safe Packet Transformation Engine with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allocate flexible-array objects with checked header-plus-elements arithmetic and record their element count.
Validate both readable source and writable destination extents before memcpy-like operations.
Trace allocation, publication, last valid use, deallocation, stale use, and address reuse to diagnose use-after-free.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Flexible Array Allocation
steps.instruction:
project case — A project native-systems team handles memory case 1663. Produce bounded C evidence to decode representation bytes through a defined copy; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
